subject: Geography
correct_response: Yes! A map shows a picture of a place!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Maps help us see where places are!
hint: Maps have arrows that show direction. The arrow points to the top of the page!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What direction is at the top of the map?
correct_response: Yes! North is at the top of the map!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Find the letter N on the compass!
hint: Look for the N at the top-left corner of the map!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call the left side of the map?
correct_response: Yes! West is on the left side of the map!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Find the W at the top-left corner!
hint: The sun rises in the east and sets in the west!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call the right side of the map?
correct_response: Yes! East is on the right side of the map!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Find the E at the top-right corner!
hint: The sun sets in the east and goes down in the west!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call the bottom of the map?
correct_response: Yes! South is at the bottom of the map!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Find the S at the bottom of the map!
hint: South is warmer than North, so it's at the bottom!

subject: Geography
correct_answers: A tool that helps us find directions
correct_response: Yes! A compass helps us find our way!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Try following the arrow!
hint: Compasses always point North. Look for the red needle!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we see on a map?
correct_response: Yes! Maps show us water, land, and countries!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Maps show different colors for different things!
hint: Blue is usually water, green is often land, yellow can be countries!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call a very high place on a map?
correct_response: Yes! Mountains are the very highest places!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Mountains are tall and have snow on top!
hint: Mountains have peaks and valleys. They're shown with brown on maps!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call a very low place on a map?
correct_response: Yes! Oceans, seas, and rivers are blue and wavy on maps!
incorrect_response: Not quite. They're large bodies of water!
hint: Oceans are the biggest water on Earth!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call a big island?
correct_response: Yes! Islands are land surrounded by water!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Islands can be big or small!
hint: Look for land completely surrounded by blue water!
